| First game          = {{nowrap|{{flagicon| British Leeward Islands|1871}} '''Saint Christopher and Nevis''' 2–4 {{fb-rt|Grenada|1903}}<br>([[Saint Kitts and Nevis|Saint Christopher and Nevis]]; 18 August 1938)}}
| Largest win         = {{fb|St. Kitts and Nevis}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|MSR}}<br>([[Basseterre]], Saint Kitts and Nevis; 17 April 1992)<br>{{nowrap|{{fb|Saint Martin}} 0–10 {{fb-rt|St. Kitts and Nevis}}}}<br>([[The Valley, Anguilla|The Valley]], Anguilla; 14 October 2018)

| World cup best      = 
| Regional name       = [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]
| Regional cup apps   = 1
| Regional cup first  = [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
| Regional cup best   = Group stage ([[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]])

{{MedalCompetition|[[Caribbean Cup]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997 Antigua and Barbuda and Saint Kitts and Nevis]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team''' is the national team of [[Saint Kitts and Nevis]], and is controlled by the [[St. Kitts and Nevis Football Association]]. They are affiliated to the [[Caribbean Football Union]] of [[CONCACAF]]. The team has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], however, they did qualify for their first [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] appearance in 2023.

They are nicknamed ''The Sugar Boyz'' due to the sugar cultivation on the island of St. Kitts.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Aloia |first1=Andrew |title=Kieran Cooney & Diego Edwards: Meet Notts County teenagers called up by St Kitts and Nevis |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/68580645 |website=[[BBC Sport]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820014913/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/68580645 |archive-date=20 August 2025 |date=19 March 2024 |quote='Sugar Boyz' - drawn from the country's history of sugar cultivation.}}</ref>

==History==

===Beginnings (1938–1990)===
Saint Kitts and Nevis played their first match on 18 August 1938, against [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]], a match that ended in a 2–4 defeat.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Courtney |first1=Barrie |title=Saint Kitts and Nevis – List of International Matches |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/stkitts-intres.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=31 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230331133042/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/stkitts-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref> They participated in the [[Leeward Islands Tournament]] from 1949 however had to wait until 1979 to play their first official matches, in the qualifying rounds for the [[1979 CFU Championship]], losing twice to [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]], both results finishing 2–1.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Cruickshank |first1=Mark |last2=Courtney |first2=Barrie |last3=Garin |first3=Erik |last4=Morrison |first4=Neil |title=CFU Championship 1979 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cfu79.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819030105/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cfu79.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=12 April 2018}}</ref> They would again fail to qualify for the final phase of the [[1983 CFU Championship]] after advancing due to [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]]'s withdrawal, eliminated by [[Martinique national football team|Martinique]], who won 12–0 on aggregate.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Cruickshank |first1=Mark |last2=Courtney |first2=Barrie |last3=Garin |first3=Erik |title=CFU Championship 1983 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cfu83.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819025710/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cfu83.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=20 December 2012}}</ref>

===1990–2000===
The ''Sugar Boyz'' qualified for their first tournament at the [[1993 Caribbean Cup]], drawing against [[Dominican Republic national football team|Dominican Republic]] (2–2) and [[British Virgin Islands national football team|British Virgin Islands]] (5–1) to top their qualifying group.<ref name="carib93" >{{cite web |last1=J. McKain |first1=Chris |title=Shell Caribbean Cup 1993 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gold-car93.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819175604/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gold-car93.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=18 July 2002}}</ref> In the tournament proper, they finished second behind [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]] to reach the semi-finals, before losing to [[Martinique national football team|Martinique]] on penalties.<ref name="carib93" /> In the match for third place, they were defeated 3–2 by [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]].<ref name="carib93" /> Saint Kitts and Nevis finished bottom of their group in [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]<ref>{{cite web |last1=J. McKain |first1=Chris |title=Shell Caribbean Cup 1996 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/shell-car96.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819175919/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/shell-car96.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=24 July 2003}}</ref> but in the [[1997 Caribbean Cup]], they advanced into the knock-out after finishing level on points with both [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] and [[Martinique national football team|Martinique]], then defeated [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]] 2–1 in extra-time<ref name="carib97" >{{cite web |last1=J. McKain |first1=Chris |title=Shell Caribbean Cup 1997 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gold-car97.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819180111/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gold-car97.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=4 March 2011}}</ref> with both goals scored by [[Keith Gumbs]],<ref name="goldcup23" >{{cite web |last1=Lewis |first1=Michael |title=2023 Concacaf Gold Cup: USMNT vs. Saint Kitts and Nevis - Match History & Preview {{!}} Five Things to Know |url=https://www.ussoccer.com/stories/2023/06/2023-concacaf-gold-cup-usmnt-vs-saint-kitts-and-nevis-match-history-preview-five-things-to-know |website=[[ussoccer.com]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819180259/https://www.ussoccer.com/stories/2023/06/2023-concacaf-gold-cup-usmnt-vs-saint-kitts-and-nevis-match-history-preview-five-things-to-know |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=26 June 2023}}</ref> but they succumbed to [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 0–4 in the final.<ref>{{cite web |title=T&T lift Shell/Umbro Cup |url=https://www.socawarriors.net/statistics.html?view=matchreport&id=267 |website=[[Jamaica Gleaner]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819180445/https://www.socawarriors.net/statistics.html?view=matchreport&id=267 |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=14 July 1997}}</ref> Since they'd reached the final, Saint Kitts and Nevis were entered into a play-off against [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] – runner-up in the [[1996 Caribbean Cup]] – for the last ticket to the [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup]], but they lost 2–0.<ref name="carib97" />

Saint Kitts and Nevis participated for the first time in the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1998 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]].<ref name="kittsnevis-wcq" >{{cite web |last1=Courtney |first1=Barrie |title=Saint Kitts and Nevis - Details of World Cup Qualifiers |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/stkitts-intres-detwcq.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820014544/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/stkitts-intres-detwcq.html |archive-date=20 August 2025 |date=20 February 2003}}</ref> After benefiting from the withdrawal of the [[Bahamas national football team|Bahamas]], they beat [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]] 5–1 in [[Basseterre]] and 1–0 in [[Castries]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Stokkermans |first1=Karel |last2=Jarreta |first2=Sergio Henrique |title=World Cup 1998 Qualifying |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/98q.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819182256/https://www.rsssf.org/tables/98q.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=12 December 2002}}</ref> but fell in the third knockout round against [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]], losing thanks to the [[away goals rule]].<ref>{{cite web |agency=Associated Press |title=JAMAICA, CUBA ADVANCE IN WORLD CUP QUALIFYING PLAY |url=https://www.deseret.com/1996/7/1/19251944/jamaica-cuba-advance-in-world-cup-qualifying-play/ |website=[[Deseret News]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819182527/https://www.deseret.com/1996/7/1/19251944/jamaica-cuba-advance-in-world-cup-qualifying-play/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=1 July 1996}}</ref>

===2000s===
In the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2002 World Cup qualifiers]], after comfortably beating the [[Turks and Caicos Islands national football team|Turks and Caicos Islands]] with an aggregate result of 14–0, [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]] once again dispatched the ''Sugar Boyz'', winning both in [[Kingstown]] (1–0) as in [[Basseterre]] (1–2).<ref name="kittsnevis-wcq" /> However, the situation would improve in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2006 qualifiers]], since Saint Kitts and Nevis advanced to the second group phase after leaving the [[US Virgin Islands national football team|US Virgin Islands]] and [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] on the way.<ref>{{cite web |agency=Associated Press |title=U.S. Looking to Dash Grenada's Faint Hopes |url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2004-jun-20-sp-usmen20-story.html |website=[[Los Angeles Times]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819190819/https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2004-jun-20-sp-usmen20-story.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=20 June 2004}}</ref> They shared Group 3 with [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]], [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] and [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]], but as expected, finished in last place, with 6 losses in as many appearances.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF) 2006, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.net/competitions/738/2006/ |website=Soccer365.net |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820011921/https://soccer365.net/competitions/738/2006/ |archive-date=20 August 2025}}</ref> They also experienced the worst defeat in their history, 8–0 against [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Pérez |first1=Salvador |title=El Tri, invicto como local fuera del Azteca en eliminatoria |url=https://www.espn.com.mx/futbol/mundial/nota/_/id/3587813/el-tri-invicto-como-local-fuera-del-azteca-en-eliminatoria |website=[[ESPN]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819223424/https://www.espn.com.mx/futbol/mundial/nota/_/id/3587813/el-tri-invicto-como-local-fuera-del-azteca-en-eliminatoria |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=3 October 2017 |trans-title=El Tri is undefeated in qualifiers played at home, but away from the Azteca}}</ref> In the preliminary round for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2010 qualifiers]], they were unable to repeat what they had done four years ago and were unceremoniously eliminated by [[Belize national football team|Belize]], who won 4–2 on aggregate.<ref>{{cite web |last1=W. Williams |first1=Lesroy |title=WHIPPING BOYS GO TO MEXICO, SUGAR BOYS STAY HOME |url=https://www.thestkittsnevisobserver.com/whipping-boys-go-mexico-sugar-boys-stay-home/ |website=[[The St. Kitts-Nevis Observer]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819185714/https://www.thestkittsnevisobserver.com/whipping-boys-go-mexico-sugar-boys-stay-home/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=28 March 2008}}</ref>

In the Caribbean Cup, the ''Sugar Boyz'' did not repeat the successes of the '90s and could only qualify to the final phase of the [[2001 Caribbean Cup]], although they did not advance beyond the group phase.<ref>{{cite web |last1=McKain |first1=Chris |last2=Díaz Rubio |first2=Julián |last3=Goloby |first3=James |title=Copa Caribe 2001 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/caribe01.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820012219/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/caribe01.html |archive-date=20 August 2025 |date=21 August 2004}}</ref> They have not returned to a final phase since that edition.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Sidway |first1=Scott |title=FC Dallas journeyman Atiba Harris finds fresh start on the field, family happiness in return to Texas |url=http://www.mlssoccer.com/news/article/2015/07/23/fc-dallas-journeyman-atiba-harris-finds-fresh-start-field-family-happiness-r |website=[[MLSsoccer.com]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150725094807/http://www.mlssoccer.com/news/article/2015/07/23/fc-dallas-journeyman-atiba-harris-finds-fresh-start-field-family-happiness-r |archive-date=25 July 2015 |date=23 July 2015 |quote=he has led the 120th-ranked Sugar Boyz for over a decade, becoming their captain and leading scorer. With the country boasting a population of just under 55,000, it's always a grind for Harris & Co., who haven't qualified for a Caribbean Cup since 2001}}</ref>

===2010–present===

[[File:Theo Wharton 2017-08-05 1.jpg|thumb|150px|right|[[Theo Wharton]] made his debut for St. Kitts and Nevis in 2016]]

Qualifying directly to the second round of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2014 qualifiers]], Saint Kitts and Nevis was drawn in Group D along with [[Canada national men's soccer team|Canada]], [[Puerto Rico national football team|Puerto Rico]] and [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]].<ref>{{cite web |title=CONCACAF announces schedule for Round 2 |url=https://www.canadasoccer.com/news/concacaf-announces-schedule-for-round-2/ |website=[[Canada Soccer Association]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200922010246/https://www.canadasoccer.com/news/concacaf-announces-schedule-for-round-2/ |archive-date=22 September 2020 |date=30 July 2011}}</ref> They finished in 3rd place, with 7 points.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF) 2014, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.net/competitions/738/2014/ |website=Soccer365.net |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819225716/https://soccer365.net/competitions/738/2014/ |archive-date=19 August 2025}}</ref> It was only defeated by [[Canada national men's soccer team|Canada]] in Toronto (4–0).<ref>{{cite web |title=Canada scores four times in season finale at BMO Field |url=https://canadasoccer.com/news/canada-scores-four-times-in-season-finale-at-bmo-field/ |website=[[Canada Soccer Association]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819224118/https://canadasoccer.com/news/canada-scores-four-times-in-season-finale-at-bmo-field/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=16 November 2011}}</ref> In the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2018 World Cup qualifiers]], Saint Kitts and Nevis defeated the [[Turks and Caicos Islands national football team|Turks and Caicos Islands]] in the first round by a lofty aggregate 12–4<ref>{{cite web |last1=Dixon |first1=Loshaun |title=Sugar Boyz repeat scoreline in Turks and Caic |url=https://www.sknvibes.com/news/newsdetails.cfm/91399 |website=SKN Vibes |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819173103/https://www.sknvibes.com/news/newsdetails.cfm/91399 |archive-date=19 August 2025 |location= [[Basseterre]] |date=27 March 2015}}</ref> before falling in the second phase, at the hands of [[El Salvador national football team|El Salvador]], 6–3 on aggregate, but not before obtaining a 2–2 draw at home.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Horne |first1=Patrick |title=FIFA'S GOOD MOVE |url=https://www.caribbeanlife.com/fifas-good-move/ |website=Caribbean Life |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820013350/https://www.caribbeanlife.com/fifas-good-move/ |archive-date=20 August 2025 |date=19 June 2015 |quote=El Salvador and St. Kitts/Nevis played to a 2-2 draw in St. Kitts, but El Salvador scored a 4-1 victory at home to finished with a 6-3 aggregate.}}</ref> In November 2015, they played 2 friendlies against European teams, [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]] (1–0) and [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] (0–3).<ref>{{cite web |last1=Oll |first1=M. |title=Estonian football team ends season with 3:0 win over Saint Kitts and Nevis |url=https://news.err.ee/117212/estonian-football-team-ends-season-with-3-0-win-over-saint-kitts-and-nevis |website=[[Eesti Rahvusringhääling]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819172838/https://news.err.ee/117212/estonian-football-team-ends-season-with-3-0-win-over-saint-kitts-and-nevis |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=17 November 2015}}</ref> [[Devaughn Elliott]] scored the only goal in the victory over Andorra, becoming the first St. Kitts and Nevis player to score against a European side.<ref name="St. Kitts and Nevis vs. Andorra">{{cite web|title=St. Kitts and Nevis vs. Andorra|url=https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2015/11/12/world/friendlies/andorra/st-kitts-and-nevis/2165974/?ICID=HP_MS_01_03|publisher=Soccerway|access-date=12 November 2015|archive-date=6 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211006133700/https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2015/11/12/world/friendlies/andorra/st-kitts-and-nevis/2165974/?ICID=HP_MS_01_03|url-status=live}}</ref> The result was also the first away victory for a [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] team over a European side on their home soil.<ref name="European Win">{{cite web|title='Sugar Boyz' defeat Andorra in historic European win|url=http://miyvue.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=24680:sugar-boyz-defeat-andorra-in-historic-european-win&catid=42:rokstories&Itemid=109|publisher=miyvue.com|access-date=14 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151117030604/http://miyvue.com/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=24680:sugar-boyz-defeat-andorra-in-historic-european-win&catid=42:rokstories&Itemid=109|archive-date=17 November 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In October 2016, it reached its highest ranking in the FIFA world ranking (73rd place)<ref>{{cite web |title=Germany, Brazil climb to close in on Argentina |url=https://inside.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/men/news/germany-brazil-climb-to-close-in-on-argentina-2844704 |website=[[FIFA.com]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819230836/https://inside.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/men/news/germany-brazil-climb-to-close-in-on-argentina-2844704 |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=20 October 2016}}</ref> thanks to its good performance in the 2nd round of the [[2017 Caribbean Cup qualification|2017 Caribbean Cup of Nations qualifiers]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Lugo |first1=Erik Francisco |title=Caribbean Championship 2016/17 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/caribe2016.html |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819231254/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/caribe2016.html |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=28 June 2017}}</ref> However, two defeats against [[French Guiana national football team|French Guiana]] (0–1)<ref>{{cite web |title=Team SKN loses to French Guiana, but still eyeing the Gold Cup |url=https://sknfa.com/team-skn-loses-to-french-guiana-but-still-eyeing-the-gold-cup/ |website=[[Saint Kitts and Nevis Football Association]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819173643/https://sknfa.com/team-skn-loses-to-french-guiana-but-still-eyeing-the-gold-cup/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=13 October 2016}}</ref> and [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]] at home (0–2 a.e.t.) stopped the ''Sugar Boyz'' in the 3rd round of these qualifiers.<ref>{{cite web |title=Haiti defeats Sugar Boyz 2-0 In Extra Time |url=https://sknpulse.com/haiti-defeats-sugar-boyz-2-0-in-extra-time/ |website=SKN Pulse |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250820013655/https://sknpulse.com/haiti-defeats-sugar-boyz-2-0-in-extra-time/ |archive-date=20 August 2025 |date=13 November 2016}}</ref> The year ends with a 1–1 draw in [[Basseterre]] against [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] on November 19, 2016, in a friendly match, one year after playing against the same team in [[Tallinn]].<ref>{{cite web |title=St. Kitts and Nevis Draw 1-1 With Estonia In International Friendly |url=https://sknpulse.com/st-kitts-and-nevis-draw-1-1-with-estonia-in-international-friendly/ |website=SKN Pulse |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819233917/https://sknpulse.com/st-kitts-and-nevis-draw-1-1-with-estonia-in-international-friendly/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=20 November 2017}}</ref>

In June 2017, the team went on a second European tour and played [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] on June 4 in [[Yerevan]]<ref name="armenia" >{{cite web |title=Henrikh Mkhitaryan at double as Armenia national team beat Saint Kitts and Nevis in a friendly match |url=https://en.aravot.am/2017/06/05/195276/ |website=[[Aravot]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819031921/https://en.aravot.am/2017/06/05/195276/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=5 June 2017}}</ref> and [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] three days later in [[Tbilisi]].<ref name="georgia" >{{cite web |last1=Hewlett |first1=LK |title=St. Kitts-Nevis Played Good Football Despite Loss To Georgia |url=https://timescaribbeanonline.com/st-kitts-nevis-played-good-football-despite-loss-georgia/ |website=Times Caribbean Online |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819032234/https://timescaribbeanonline.com/st-kitts-nevis-played-good-football-despite-loss-georgia/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=8 June 2017}}</ref> Both games ended in equally bad losses, 5–0 and 3–0, respectively.<ref name="armenia" /><ref name="georgia" /> St. Kitts and Nevis continued its pattern of international tours and travels in August 2017 to [[Mumbai]] to meet [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] and [[India men's national football team|India]] in a friendly tournament, the [[2017 Hero Tri-Nation Series]]. Both matches ended in draws acquired by the same score of one goal each.<ref>{{cite web |author1=PTI |author1-link=Press Trust of India |title=St Kitts and Nevis hold Mauritius to 1-1 draw in tri-nation football tournament |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/sports/football/st-kitts-and-nevis-hold-mauritius-to-1-1-draw-in-tri-nation-football-tournament-4808964/ |website=[[The Indian Express]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819031125/https://indianexpress.com/article/sports/football/st-kitts-and-nevis-hold-mauritius-to-1-1-draw-in-tri-nation-football-tournament-4808964/ |archive-date=19 August 2025 |date=22 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author1=Press Trust of India |author1-link=Press Trust of India |title=India held 1-1 by St Kitts & Nevis but win Tri-Nation tournament |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/football/top-stories/india-held-1-1-by-st-kitts-nevis-but-win-tri-nation-tournament/articleshow/60213810.cms |website=[[The Times of India]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819031234/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/football/top-stories/india-held-1-1-by-st-kitts-nevis-but-win-tri-nation-tournament/articleshow/60213810.cms |archive-date=19 August 2025 |location=[[Mumbai]] |date=24 August 2017}}</ref>

The ''Sugar Boyz'' played [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]] again on March 25, 2022, six and a half years after their first confrontation, for a friendly match in [[Andorra la Vella]]. This time the Principality's selection won against Saint Kitts and Nevis on its home stadium (1–0).<ref>{{cite web |title=Andorra-St Kitts and Nevis |url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2034632--andorra-vs-st-kitts-and-nevis/ |website=[[UEFA.com]] |access-date=19 August 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250819032534/https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2034632--andorra-vs-st-kitts-and-nevis/ |archive-date=19 August 2025}}</ref>

They qualified for their first ever CONCACAF Gold Cup in [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]].<ref name="goldcup23" />

==Coaches==

[[File:Leo neiva1.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Leonardo Neiva]] became the manager of St. Kitts and Nevis in 2021]]

{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Carlos Cavagnaro]] (1988)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Alistair Edwards]] (1996–1997)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Ces Podd]] (1999–2000)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Clinton Percival]] (2000–2001)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Elvis Browne]] (2002–2004)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Lenny Lake]] (2004)
*{{flagicon|USA}} [[Leonard Taylor (football manager)|Leonard Taylor]] (2008)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Lester Morris]] (2008)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Lenny Lake]] (2008–2010)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Clinton Percival]] (2010–2012)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Jeffrey Hazel]] (2012–2015)
*{{flagicon|MEX}} [[Jacques Passy]] (2015–2019)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Earl Jones (football coach)|Earl Jones]] (2019)
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Claudio Caimi]] (2019–2021)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Leonardo Neiva]] (2021)
*{{flagicon|SKN}} [[Austin Huggins]] (2022–2023)
*{{flagicon|MEX}} [[Francisco Molina (football coach)|Francisco Molina]] (2023–2025)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Marcelo Serrano]] (2025–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]] matches against [[Indonesia national football team|Indonesia]] on 27 March and [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] on 30 March 2026.<ref>{{cite web |title=St. Kitts and Nevis Announces Squad for FIFA Series matches |url=https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1BuuQG3kYj/ |publisher=[[St. Kitts and Nevis Football Association]] |access-date=22 March 2026 |date=21 November 2026}}</ref>

:''Caps and goals correct as of 30 March 2026, after the match against [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Xander Parke]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|11|17|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Hednesford Town F.C.|Hednesford Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=18|pos=GK|name=[[Julani Archibald]]|age={{Birth date and age|1991|5|18|df=y}}|caps=79|goals=0|club=[[Valletta F.C.|Valletta]]|clubnat=MLT}}

{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Malique Roberts]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|8|1|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=1|club=[[Cayon Rockets]]|clubnat=SKN}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Ethan Bristow]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|11|27|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=1|club=[[Tranmere Rovers F.C.|Tranmere Rovers]]|clubnat=ENG}}

{{Nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Yohannes Mitchum]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|4|6|df=y}}|caps=53|goals=1|club=[[Cayon Rockets]]|clubnat=SKN}}

{{nat fs end}}
